perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information shall not include information that (a) is or becomes available to the public other than as a result of acts by the Receiving Party in breach of the terms of this Agreement, (b) was in the Receiving Party’s or its Representatives’ possession nor to disclosure by the Company, (c) is disclosed to the Receiving Party or its Representatives by a third party not known by the Receiving Party or its Representatives to be bound by any duty or obligation of confidentiality on a non-confidential basis to the Company with regards to the information or (d) is independently developed by you or your Representatives without violating your obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information shall not include information that (a) is or becomes available to the public other than as a direct result of acts by the Receiving Party in breach of the terms of this Agreement, (b) was in the Receiving Party’s or its Representatives’ possession prior to disclosure by the Company, demonstrated by written records, (c) is disclosed to the Receiving Party or its Representatives by a third party not known by the Receiving Party or its Representatives to be bound by any duty or obligation of confidentiality on a non-confidential basis to the Company with regards to the information or (d) is independently developed by you or your Representatives without violating your obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'direct' to (a), we create a contradiction because Section 7 refers to disclosure required by law, which might indirectly lead to information becoming public. By adding 'demonstrated by written records' to (b) we are contradicting any prior verbal agreements or information the representatives had which may not have any documentations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure Pursuant to Law, Regulation, Subpoena or Court Order. In the event that the Receiving Party or anyone to whom it discloses the Confidential Information receives a request to disclose all or any part of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information under the terms of a subpoena or other order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or by another governmental agency or as 3 otherwise required pursuant to law, regulation or other legal process, the Receiving Party shall (if legally practicable or permitted) (a) promptly notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (b) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking steps to resist or narrow such request, (c) if disclosure of such Confidential Information or Discussion Information is required, furnish only such portion of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information as the Receiving Party is advised by outside legal counsel is legally required to be disclosed and (d) take commercially reasonable efforts to cooperate with the Company (at the Company’s expense) in its efforts to obtain a protective order or other relief to prevent the disclosure of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to such portion of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information, as applicable, that is required to be disclosed. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party or its Representatives will be permitted to disclose the Confidential Information or any portion thereof upon the routine request of any government or regulatory body having or claiming to have authority to regulate or oversee any aspect of your or your Representatives’ business of that of its affiliates, provided that they shall advise the governmental or regulatory body of the confidential nature of such information.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'direct' in the Changed Text (a) creates a contradiction with the Contradicted Text, which allows for disclosure required by law, regulation, subpoena or court order. Disclosure pursuant to these legal obligations might indirectly lead to information becoming public, which now contradicts the 'direct' requirement in the Changed Text. Also, by adding 'demonstrated by written records' to (b) in the Changed Text, we are contradicting any prior verbal agreements or information the representatives had which may not have any documentations. The original text allows for prior knowledge regardless of documentation, whereas the changed text requires written record. The contradicted text does not mention anything about written records, making them contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or the breach of the terms of this Agreement applicable to Representatives by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall only be responsible for any direct breach of the terms of this Agreement by the Receiving Party and not for the breach of the terms of this Agreement applicable to Representatives by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The change specifies the Receiving Party is only responsible for its own direct breaches, not those of its Representatives. This is an in-text contradiction to the later clause of section 3a, which states, 'The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or the breach of the terms of this Agreement applicable to Representatives by any of its Representatives.', leading to uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall, and shall direct its Representatives to, keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) the Receiving Party may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information to which the Company gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible cooperation and who agree to keep such information in confidence to the same extent as described herein. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or the breach of the terms of this Agreement applicable to Representatives by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Receiving Party is NOT responsible for breaches by its Representatives, whereas the contradicted text states that the Receiving Party IS responsible for such breaches. This is a direct and irreconcilable conflict, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'. The change narrows the Receiving Party's liability, breaking compatibility with the broader liability outlined in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information to (i) divert or attempt to divert any known business or customer of the Company or (ii) solicit for employment, or initiate contact for employment with, any known employee of the Company; provided, however, nothing will prohibit: (i) recruiting efforts that are not based on confidential information or general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not specifically targeting employees of the Company and the hiring as a result, (ii) the solicitation and hiring of any individual who is no longer employed by the Company at the time of such solicitation or hiring and (iii) the hiring by you of anyone who initiates contact with you regarding such employment.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of six (6) months from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information to (i) divert or attempt to divert any known business or customer of the Company or (ii) solicit for employment, or initiate contact for employment with, any known employee of the Company; provided, however, nothing will prohibit: (i) recruiting efforts that are not based on confidential information or general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not specifically targeting employees of the Company and the hiring as a result, (ii) the solicitation and hiring of any individual who is no longer employed by the Company at the time of such solicitation or hiring and (iii) the hiring by you of anyone who initiates contact with you regarding such employment.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the non-compete period from one year to six months, there's now an contradiction with Section 18, which states 'This Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect one (1) year from the date hereof.' This introduces ambiguity regarding how long the non-compete clause actually lasts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect one (1) year from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a one-year non-compete period, aligning with the contradicted text stating the agreement terminates after one year. The changed text reduces the non-compete period to six months, creating a contradiction with the contradicted text that states the agreement lasts for one year. This introduces ambiguity regarding the actual duration of the non-compete obligation, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return or destroy, at its sole option, all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect to a substantial degree any Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 6, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term hereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, any return or destruction is subject to law, regulation and internal document retention policies.
perturbation.changed_text: Within 30 days of the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return or destroy, at its sole option, all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect to a substantial degree any Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 6, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will only be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement related to legal proceedings for one year after returning or destroying the documents, not for the term hereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, any return or destruction is subject to law, regulation and internal document retention policies.
perturbation.explanation: By adding a specific timeframe (30 days) to the return of information and limiting confidentiality obligations to legal proceedings for one year after, we create a conflict. The original text implies indefinite confidentiality, but the edited text limits it. This contradicts the original intention of continuous confidentiality, causing potential legal dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return or destroy, at its sole option, all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect to a substantial degree any Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 6, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term hereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, any return or destruction is subject to law, regulation and internal document retention policies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement to limit the confidentiality obligations to legal proceedings for one year, while the contradicted text explicitly states that confidentiality obligations continue for the term of the agreement. This creates a direct conflict and in-text contradiction, as the two sections prescribe different durations for the same obligation. According to the Perturbation Explanation and Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction definition, different sections of a contract providing conflicting obligations constitutes an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing or anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the Company acknowledges and agrees that this Agreement is in no way intended to restrict Receiving Party’s (or its Representatives’) ability to trade in securities or instruments (whether physical or derivative) of the Company or any of its affiliates or subsidiaries.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing or anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the Company acknowledges and agrees that this Agreement is in no way intended to restrict Receiving Party’s (or its Representatives’) ability to trade in securities or instruments (whether physical or derivative) of the Company or any of its affiliates or subsidiaries, unless such trading is based on Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase 'unless such trading is based on Confidential Information,' we introduce a conditional restriction on securities trading. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 11, which states explicitly that the agreement isn't intended to restrict trading. Adding this exclusion creates ambiguity regarding permissible trading activity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Securities Laws . The Receiving Party acknowledges that it is aware and that the Receiving Party and its Representatives have been advised that the United States securities laws may prohibit any person having non-public material information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of that company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities. Notwithstanding the foregoing or anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the Company acknowledges and agrees that this Agreement is in no way intended to restrict Receiving Party’s (or its Representatives’) ability to trade in securities or instruments (whether physical or derivative) of the Company or any of its affiliates or subsidiaries.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the agreement is 'in no way intended to restrict Receiving Party’s ability to trade.' The changed text adds 'unless such trading is based on Confidential Information,' which introduces a restriction on trading. This directly contradicts the original assertion and creates an inconsistency within the contract, as highlighted in the Perturbation Explanation and the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect one (1) year from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect two (2) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By extending the termination date of the agreement, we create a conflict. While most clauses now extend for 2 years, Section 3(b), which states 'The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information to (i) divert or attempt to divert any known business or customer of the Company or (ii) solicit for employment, or initiate contact for employment with, any known employee of the Company', introduces ambiguity regarding how long the non-compete clause actually lasts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information to (i) divert or attempt to divert any known business or customer of the Company or (ii) solicit for employment, or initiate contact for employment with, any known employee of the Company; provided, however, nothing will prohibit: (i) recruiting efforts that are not based on confidential information or general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not specifically targeting employees of the Company and the hiring as a result, (ii) the solicitation and hiring of any individual who is no longer employed by the Company at the time of such solicitation or hiring and (iii) the hiring by you of anyone who initiates contact with you regarding such employment.
perturbation.justification: The original agreement terminates after one year, and Section 3(b) restricts the Receiving Party for one year. The changed agreement terminates after two years, but Section 3(b) still only restricts the Receiving Party for one year, creating an inconsistency because the agreement exists for two years, but the Receiving party is only restricted for the first year. This represents a clear conflict because the duration of the agreement now exceeds the duration of the non-compete clause within it, thus creating an in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
